# Pay-per-Pick Inquiry — {tech: europe}

From: Bela Wirtz, {tech: europe}
To: UNIFI Deal Desk
Subject: Inquiry for Pay-per-Pick offer — trophy assembly line

Dear UNIFI team,

We at {tech: europe} are evaluating a Pay-per-Pick arrangement for the final-assembly line of one of our trophy products. Our company manufactures premium trophies and corporate awards; the line in question handles a single signature trophy model with three major sub-components.

The line will run 6 robots, performing pick-and-place of trophy components: pulling each part from a feeder station and placing it onto the assembly jig. We expect roughly 120 000 picks per month at base volume — significantly lower throughput than e-commerce work because every placement is precise and the parts are heavy.

The three component weights for this trophy are: 5 kg, 10 kg, and 15 kg. The mix on the line is roughly 50 percent of the 5 kg parts, 30 percent of the 10 kg parts, and 20 percent of the 15 kg parts.

Our seasonality is event-driven. We have two clear peaks: late spring (May to early July) for end-of-season sports tournaments and championship awards, and the November to mid-December window for corporate year-end awards. Each peak runs about 6 weeks at roughly +70 percent of base volume.

We would prefer a 4-year contract term. Flexibility is medium-priority for us — the awards business is event-coupled, and we want a clean way to ramp capacity up during peaks without locking ourselves into fixed leasing payments year-round.

Please advise on the suitable robot type given our load profile, and provide a structured offer covering price per pick, expected monthly cost at base and at peak, recommended contract clauses, and a clear cash-flow comparison against a classical leasing arrangement.

Best regards,
Bela Wirtz
{tech: europe}
